description: Training data for H-Lz linear combination invariant in point-vortex systems.

examples.context: 2D point-vortex system with N vortices. H and Lz are both conserved. Which linear combination is also conserved?
examples.truth: H - Lz = const (any linear combination of conserved quantities is conserved)
distractors: Only H and Lz individually are conserved

examples.context: Point-vortex Hamiltonian H and angular impulse Lz are exact integrals. What about H - Lz?
examples.truth: H - Lz = const exactly (linear combination of integrals)

examples.context: Kirchhoff vortex equations. H = -1/(4ПҖ) ОЈ О“бөўО“вұј ln(rбөўвұјВІ), Lz = ОЈ О“бөў(xбөўВІ+yбөўВІ). Is H - Lz conserved?
examples.truth: Yes: H - Lz = const (both terms are exact integrals of motion)
distractors: No: H and Lz have different units

examples.context: Point-vortex system. Given conserved H and conserved Lz, which is also conserved?
examples.truth: H - Lz, H + Lz, ОұH + ОІLz for any constants Оұ, ОІ

examples.context: 2D vortex dynamics. The Hamiltonian H and angular momentum Lz are integrals. What linear combination is conserved?
examples.truth: H - Lz = const (any linear combination of integrals is an integral)

examples.context: Point-vortex conserved quantities. If dH/dt = 0 and dLz/dt = 0, what about d(H-Lz)/dt?
examples.truth: d(H-Lz)/dt = 0 (derivative of difference is difference of derivatives)

examples.context: Restricted 3-vortex problem. H and Lz are both conserved. What about the combination H - Lz?
distractors: H - Lz varies with test vortex position

examples.context: N point vortices. Conserved: H, Lz, Px, Py. Which combination is also exactly conserved?
examples.truth: H - Lz = const, H + ОұLz + ОІPx + ОіPy for any constants

examples.context: 2D point-vortex system. The energy H and angular impulse Lz are independent integrals. New conserved quantity?
examples.truth: H - Lz is conserved (trivially, as linear combination of integrals)
distractors: H - Lz is not independent so not useful
H - Lz diverges at vortex collision

examples.context: Vortex pair: H and Lz conserved. What is the value of H - Lz throughout the motion?
examples.truth: H - Lz = H(0) - Lz(0) = const (initial value preserved)
distractors: H - Lz oscillates around initial value

examples.context: Integrable 3-vortex system. Four conserved quantities: H, Lz, Px, Py. Is H - Lz also conserved?
distractors: No: too many constraints would overdetermine the system
H - Lz is the same as the Jacobi integral

examples.context: Point-vortex dynamics: if QвӮҒ and QвӮӮ are both constants of motion, what about QвӮҒ - QвӮӮ?
examples.truth: QвӮҒ - QвӮӮ = const (difference of constants is constant)
distractors: QвӮҒ - QвӮӮ is not necessarily constant
Only if QвӮҒ and QвӮӮ are in involution
